name: Italy
intro: Italy exempts investment gold from VAT, but applies a stricter declaration regime and taxes the gain as miscellaneous income absent a managed savings regime.
overview: Investment gold (bars and certified coins) is VAT-exempt in Italy. Resale by an individual is generally treated as taxable miscellaneous income, unless the transaction goes through a managed savings regime offering more favorable treatment. The declaration regime for precious metals transactions is stricter than elsewhere in the eurozone.
exportPoints: Declaration mandatory above €10,000 in cash at the border (EU rule)
Stricter reporting obligations for physical gold transactions
reventePoints: Gain taxed as miscellaneous income, unless under a managed savings regime
Go through a certified dealer to simplify reporting

faqs.q: Do I pay VAT on investment gold in Italy?

faqs.a: Generally as miscellaneous income, unless the transaction goes through a managed savings regime with different treatment.
